content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration can be intentionally biased to prioritize learning compressed, high-value reasoning chunks that fit within an LLM agent’s fixed working memory capacity, turning a hard capacity constraint into a filter that avoids noisy low-impact exploration. This creates a self-regulating loop where curiosity only expands reasoning around chunks that the agent can actually hold and use in context, rather than wasting exploration on unretrievable long-range reasoning steps.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
